meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Template to Solve Deployment Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment bottlenecks. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS apps in record time.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure is a workflow bottleneck, not a technical dead end. By utilizing the Antigravity project template, you standardize your infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and environment variables. Combined with Claude Code, you automate the deployment process, ensuring your client projects ship reliably every time without manual configuration headaches.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on a client deadline. You've built a beautiful, functional application, but your local environment isn't talking to the server, environment variables are misconfigured, and the deployment logs are a wall of cryptic red text. It is the silent killer of freelance careers—the inability to reliably move code from machine to production.

This isn't a failure of your coding ability; it is a failure of your scaffolding. Professional shipping requires a system that removes the 'it worked on my machine' excuse. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you replace manual configuration with pre-validated, production-ready architecture that treats deployment as a solved problem.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Standardize your project structure immediately to eliminate configuration drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity production-ready template with Vercel integration and standard environment variable management.

steps.title: Configure Environment Security
steps.description: Ensure your production keys are injected securely without hardcoding secrets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project environment variables for production security using the Antigravity secret management pattern.

steps.title: Automate CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Set up automated testing and deployment triggers that fire on every push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for the current Antigravity template that runs linting, tests, and deployment on merge to main.

steps.title: Validate Deployment Health
steps.description: Create a health check endpoint to ensure your deployment is live and responding correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a /health API route that verifies database connectivity and environment variable presence within the Antigravity project structure.

steps.title: Execute First Production Ship
steps.description: Perform the final push and verify the live deployment status via the CLI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Prepare the deployment package and trigger the production build sequence for the Antigravity project.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file in your repo; Antigravity templates handle this, but verify it manually to prevent client onboarding friction.
Use Claude Code to write custom post-deploy hooks that ping your Slack channel when a client project goes live.
Never deploy directly from your local machine; always rely on the CI/CD pipeline defined in the template to ensure production parity.
Keep your build logs in a centralized folder within your project to allow Claude Code to analyze past failure patterns instantly.
Use the Antigravity 'dry-run' deployment flag to preview infrastructure changes before committing to a production build.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, document the process for your client and store the configuration in your personal 'Freelancer Playbook' to reuse for your next high-ticket project.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your infrastructure and start shipping value to your clients. With the Antigravity template and Claude Code, you are no longer just a coder; you are a deployment architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
